title: Augment with FastF1 track-status and weather regime features
summary: Official F1 strategy coverage explicitly notes Safety Car windows and low-grip/wet conditions as cases where pit decisions shift from normal stint logic.
rationale: Official F1 strategy coverage explicitly notes Safety Car windows and low-grip/wet conditions as cases where pit decisions shift from normal stint logic. FastF1 exposes track status and weather channels, giving a practical way to tag laps near yellow/VSC/SC/red-flag transitions or changing rain conditions that can trigger opportunistic next-lap stops.
implementation_hint: For each race-year, pull FastF1 `track_status_data`, `weather_data`, and race-control messages; align by lap/time to derive `is_SC`, `is_VSC`, `laps_since_status_change`, `rainfall`, `track_temp`, `air_temp`, and `status_change_next_1_2_laps` proxies. Also add race-level counts like prior neutralizations and whether the current lap lies in a historically cheap-stop window. Test both direct features and a specialist model only on wet/intermediate/wet-compound rows.
expected_effect: Can capture stop spikes that pure lap-time/tyre-life features miss, especially for wet-weather swaps and cheap stops under neutralization.
risk: Time alignment is fiddly, and public FastF1 telemetry availability or race-name normalization may be inconsistent for some sessions.
